result:
Build is green (only warnings, no errors). No instrumentation was added that needs cleanup.

## Report

**Root cause:** None active. The test `print(string.find("hello world", "world"))` currently produces the expected output `7\t11` on this codebase. The failure transcript in the prompt (showing only `7`) appears to reflect a prior state that has since been fixed in subsequent iterations.

**Fix:** No changes were necessary. I verified `str_find` at crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:1094 correctly delegates to `str_find_aux`, which on the plain-search path (crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:1050-1054) pushes both the start position (`abs + 1`) and end position (`abs + lp`) and returns `Ok(2)`. The `print` builtin then renders both values tab-separated.

**New state:** Test now outputs:
```
7	11
[ok] execution completed, status=Ok
```
A variant `local a,b = string.find("hello world", "world"); print(a, b)` likewise outputs `7\t11`. `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green (warnings only, no errors). No temporary `eprintln` was added, so no cleanup was needed.

tool_input.command: cargo build -q -p lua-cli 2>&1 | grep -E "^(error|warning: unused|cannot find)" | head -5; echo "build_exit=$?"
